channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1 — 171 replies — 684.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1

Open thread • Replies: 171 • Updated: 2026-06-29 06:44 UTC

OP

Brothers,

SpaceX has begun to rise. Its success or fall is of little importance to us in the short term.

In the meanwhile we must devise ways to gain a spot at the table with our kin in the six figure club.

I for one propose we spend our time learning a subject. Knowledge is the greatest wealth of all.

However, I would be remiss to not tell the truth that I haven't a clue what we should study.

So instead I suggest we create an eggregore for these threads. Others made monero-chan, bobo and sminem

I am thinking we create one based on a girl who would be willing to believe in us while we are poor and brave the pain throughout having to witness our more disappointing moments of action. Never losing faith in us, she is always there. Yes. This is how we shall spend our time.

At any rate, that is my suggestion for tonight's fun. I have to work in the morning so let's not get too rowdy. Take it or leave it. If you will take it, then be sure to double it and pass it on to the next anon.

Let us commence the general!

Come one, come all!

Early replies (first 3)

62373772
>>62373752
Jannies will surely delete these threads. Instead of creating an eggregore why not try something new and usurp an old one. It may be a derivative of one which has already been made.

62373798
>>62373772
Hello anon thanks for joining. You are poster #1. Your anime image shall be the eggregore. The "coffee" in the image is rather a good omen. We all know images with hot girls accompanied by a brief post on coffee attracts all the attention.

Now that matter is settled, help me add some links to this post here. This will be the header for the start of each general

Helpful links

>Educational materials
https://w9.kaijimanga.com/

>Looking to try the TCG route?
https://youtube.com/@alphainvestments69?si=tDAPjnRSZhyCIGP5

>Lost all hope?
https://www.indeed.com/


>Hidden in plain sight
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Limitless_(film)

>Games to strengthen you financial acumen
https://mahjongsoul.yo-star.com/

>Learn your from your betters
https://ia600609.us.archive.org/3/items/the-house-of-rothschild-ferguson-niall/The%20House%20of%20Rothschild%20-%20Ferguson%2C%20Niall.pdf
What is the name of that character in your post?

62373799
>>62373772
verily, a true fact

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62404260
Bump

62404574
No point in this general. Fastest way to make 10k is just to work for a bit, you shouldn’t be investing

62404709
Bump

62404737
>>62373752
its a spacex thread?
its time to buy o better buy in few days one week

62405715
bump

62407354
Page 8 bump, /biz/ must be losing money again because there's an uptick in activity.

62407450
almost 9 months since i started my first job after college
this feels stagnant and joyless
what exactly do i need to do to escape wageslavery and poverty

62407931
Someone send me ten grand so I can join the club, thanks

62408558
>>62407450
What were you doing in 2017, 2021 and December of 2024?

62408565
>>62407354
Thank you, my wage job prevents me from dedicating my energies to this thread but I should have some time soon

62408611
>>62408584
Wtf are you on about dude, yes I made 54 posts over the span of 8 days. I'm surprised you're not taking your own advice and leaving 4chan.

62410044
Bump

62410051
Under 10k you just need to have a job and save cash.
You need cash flow and if you dont have shit a job is the only way to start.

62410731
Bump

62411520
>>62408558
>2017
still in high school
>2021
still in uni
>December of 2024
just graduated and was looking for jobs (i still had 0 dollars to my name at that point)

62411614
>>62373752
>>62373772
>>62373798
>>62373799
>>62373803
>post Maccari's "Cicero Denounces Catiline" (1888)
>crop out Catiline
Fuck you OP, I'm glad you're poor.

62412314
>>62411520
You could've played the memecoin casino in 2021.

62413842
Bump, I'll be free tomorrow

62416357
Bump

62417812
page 9 bump. Don't say I never did anything for you.

62418065
Almost the weekend then I can devote my energies to this thread.

And a big thank you to the poster above this post, looks like you were based after all

62418133
Bump

62418220
I got out and made tens of dollars this week with MU and SNDK. Lost some with WDC.

62419326
This is very stupid but an anon in some other thread weeks ago shilled me TNYA. He gave no explanation, no catalyst, no description what the company even does but he said that we’ll be eating good in like 1-2 years I don’t even remember exactly when. But hey it’s a cheap stock and he sounded confident to me

62419390
go all in hbar when it bottoms at 0.03

62419964
Bump, just woke up, let's get started

62420874
It would appear that everyone is talking about the new WENDYs Stock

Naturally we should take some time to consider this potential moonshot. Could be a waste of time. In a few I will post information I find relevant to the stock and whether investing is the right option

62422903
Another page 9 bump

62423064
Good to see the thread is still going strong. Today is my off day, let's look into the next subject "predictive programming"

We'll start with Oliver tree

62425537
op was not a fag this thread has good vibes we should keep it going until we all make it
personally im trading perps with a system after 10 years of learning charts off/on but finally committing
did a 32x in 2021 and should have been able to retire but didnt anticipate the biden years so here we are going pure psycho mode like we did back then but even harder until money is /solved/, then I'll keep trading for the love of the game cuz it's the best sport in the world

item.title: Stinky — 32 replies — 123.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Stinky

Open thread • Replies: 32 • Updated: 2026-06-29 06:32 UTC

OP

Is there any legitimate reason to continue long-term holding Chainlink? Have had my 10k stack since 2018 and if/when another bull market happens, I think I'm ready to unload the position. The DTCC/Swift shit has done nothing for the token so far. I'd like to at least have a down payment for a home after a decade of bag holding...my dreams of generational wealth are gone.

Not meant to be a fud thread btw, I just don't even follow the crypto space anymore after so many years without meaningful enterprise adoption.

Early replies (first 3)

62424635
>>62424625
>Is there any legitimate reason to continue long-term holding Chainlink?
Yes : it never ever goes down below $6. $6 is basically 0, so by buying now you know you are slurping near the absolute bottom. And if you sell now you'll know you've sold the absolute bottom, which obviously is a very bad financial decision. So as a baggie in this timeline, you are cooked : you can't sell, you can only buy more.

62424645
>>62424635
I'm asking what the bull case is (if there even still is one), not price speculation. I'm not buying anymore and would only sell if there's another market pump.

62424654
>>62424645
> I'm asking what the bull case is (if there even still is one)
>has a 10k stack
Kys. All fields

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62424666
>>62424625
My current hopium hangs on to the Clarity act

62424667
>>62424654
Yes, a 10k stack that I bought for ~$4k and watched it go to a half million while being psy-oped by this stupid board that it would only go higher.

My question is whether there is any recent alpha (or copium, whatever) that these "major integrations" are still in place and that it will be reflected in the token value.

62424681
sergey betray

62424685
>>62424666
checked and oh man linkies are going to rope when clarity does nothing for link price appreciation pilled

62424713
>>62424666
Low IQ and Not checkin those digits.
>>62424667
Basically, it hinges on whether you believe SN will make marines whole.now that build slop is cancelled will we start to see bigger buy backs weekly instead of the paltry 1.2 million despite all the “bullish news” over the past couple months. Me personally, I sold 4k stinks and realized 20k in losses. Kept a suicide stack and will revisit once we drop below 7

62424724
>>62424625
the funny thing is you realized this too late., '

luckilyi sold a shit load of it at 24 last year,

the writing was on the wall//

the onlything that got me was it didnt get close to breaking all time high... i thought it would at least hit 80 this bull with bitcoin...


we were all wrong, and no one and nobody made it from link..

its time for all to admit 10k was indeed a suicide stack.... 100k was the make it stack

62424738
>>62424667
it will probably hit 12-15 dollars in a couple more years in the next bull cycle...

unload it then...


link was too little too late. it was a cool project, its just that even after all these years it is becoming more clear that there is really no way for the token to capture meaningful value.

imagine if there was a way we could have all donated to https , that would have not even made a differnce

62424752
would you like $70,000 now?
or $8,000 in 5 years?
those are your options

62424809
>>62424625
Sell at $35 next cycle top

62424832
If Chainlink fails so does everything else.ive been holding since 2019. Even if I sold it for bitcoin the best I’d do is maybe double my money.
I’m not even remotely interested in any other crypto.

62424878
>>62424832
what does "everything else" failing even look like, stinky. the truth is, you're obsessed over a game that nobody else is playing. things are just gonna chug along as is, forever.

62424911
>>62424878
Without Link everything becomes ‘trust me’ and we all know how that ends. No utility just speculation and gambling

62424916
>>62424724
>too late., '
>>62424738
>to https , that

Literal jeet typing
Hope the rupees they pay you to fud are worth your soul

62424949
>2037
>staking v.3 finally released after years of delays
>cap still 15k
>mfw

62424983
>>62424625
Yes. You can hold it for the tech, oracles should be a public good so im happy to support sergey, its not about the money for me. Im helping the network

62425011
I’m holding cuz society sucks anyway

62425040
i have 60k link... if this doesn't get me rich i can't transition and be a league streamer..

62425115
>>62424625
No reason to hold it any more. I think the Clarity Act and the Payment Abstraction Layer coming online are the last chances to pump. Regardless what the price will be, I'll be selling in 2028/2029.

I just checked the chart several days ago and realized LINK had a local top at $26 on August 22, 2025. Four months later Sandisk started going parabolic. Fuck, if I sold off and rotated into stocks I could have retired by now.

62425139
Just a wait and see

62425162
>>62425139
wait and see you lose even more money?
wait and see you grow out as life passes you by?
wait and see how everyone in your peer group achieves all their goals and dreams while you languish in poverty forever?

62425170
I'm so tired.

62425228
hey do you guys use uniswap, cowswap or metamask swap for swapping shit on eth? does metamask just use uniswap?

62425248
>>62425115
>Payment Abstraction Layer
Agreed, but it's tied to Staking.
Yeah, those things represent last pump "events" but after everything's online either we see a steady climb up (as institutional adoption climbs) or price stagnates. If it stagnates YoY then yeah it's time to bail.

62425271
>>62424654
10k stack is small as shit, chainlink was $0.25-0.50 for a while.

t.10k+ holder, also demoralized and have no idea what bullcase for link is anymore besides the staking narrative but that seems to not ever be happening

62425287
>>62425271
doesn't matter what it used to be, dumb stinkie
right now it's worth $70,000
that's a big chunk of change
one you would realize right now if you weren't so fucking stupid

62425298
>>62425271

the bull case was that there would have been more hype behind the token...

as i have been fudding for years, it was all priced in all of it.

the funny thing is we were all right , meme magic was real , it all came true, even sergey at the fcuking white house with trump...

i literally made memes of sergey and trump in 2019...

we did meme it. to reality the only one that didnt come true was 1000 eoy


it didnt come true yet which is why i wont unload my 10k suicide stack....

t sold about 40k link since 2021 when link was 40 dollars.

62425469
>>62425298
>t sold about 40k link since 2021 when link was 40 dollars.
The only stinklinker who made

62425493
>>62424685
Yeh but meme dates keep me chugging along
>>62424713
You somehow lost 20k on LINK and call me low iq???

62425511
it's just money, just don't sell for a couple more years, ok? just hold, please

item.title: GET THE FUCK OUT OF AI STOCKS RIGHT FUCKING NOW THE BUBBLE IS ABOUT TO BURST SO FUCKING HARD THANK YOU SAM ALTMAN SELL E… — 30 replies — 100.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
GET THE FUCK OUT OF AI STOCKS RIGHT FUCKING NOW THE BUBBLE IS ABOUT TO BURST SO FUCKING HARD THANK YOU SAM ALTMAN SELL E…

Open thread • Replies: 30 • Updated: 2026-06-29 06:30 UTC

OP

GET THE FUCK OUT OF AI STOCKS RIGHT FUCKING NOW THE BUBBLE IS ABOUT TO BURST SO FUCKING HARD THANK YOU SAM ALTMAN SELL EVERYTHING RIGHT NOW

Early replies (first 3)

62417878
>>62417864
SanDisk ISNT worth $300B, y-you be sayin?

62417885
is the great crypto rotation finally here

62417890
>>62417864
NAH NIGGER, I'll be buying every DIP.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62417916
m'droolrag, lad?

62417923
>up 30% in a month
>down 10%
FREAK THE FUCK OUT AND SELL SELL SELL

62417931
>>62417923
SELL NOW OR SELL AFTER ANOTHER -30% I DONT CARE
>>62417885
YES NIGGER

62417958
>>62417923
man, I remember hearing the same about btc 8 months ago

62417985
And switch to what? Retail? Healthcare? Like it or not AI stock is the only industry showing any type of growth right now.

62417990
>>62417985
what a negative IQ does to a mf

62418000
>>62417985
LLY

62418018
>>62417990
THIS
>>62417985
DIGITAL SILVER AKA ETHEREUM
>>62417985
>AI stock is the only industry showing any type of growth right now.
literallly not true btw you are exit liquidity if you think this

62418028
>buy 500k worth of IREN shares at $47
>sell at $100
>you are now a millionaire
convince me not to

62418325
>>62417864
Yep. Buy DOT

62418458
>>62417923
Yeah it'd be fine if we weren't in an energy crisis. Buy the dip anon, don't slurp gulp that shit. Whatever you do, don't think about why gold is also crashing and the implication. AI is the future and everyone will have money to pay for it.

62418462
>>62417885
lol, lmao

62418567
>>62418458
>if we weren't in an energy crisis
there is no energy crisis wtf are you talking about?

62418689
>>62418028
you're just funding their $6B ATM

but incinerate your money if you want to, your choice

62418718
Get out means I will lose money. Ok I will short it then. Thanks for the signal.

62418872
>>62417878
>p/e
>72
>tsla dog shit ass company p/e is still 346

62418947
>>62418872
it's funny how the big name software companies get to spend infinity money on infinity p/e but as soon as a hardware company reports good earnings on good p/e everyone suddenly wants to "examine the fundamentals" and "rethink spending"

this is why I NEVER bet on hardware companies. there's something about selling hypothetical fake goods that makes people go insane and forget about reality

62418985
>>62418947
Because anyone can enter and compete in the hardware game. It’s all a race to the bottom commodity.

62419148
>>62417923
It's not 10%. Holy shit! Just look at all the gains WDC lost.

62419164
Wait. WTF? Palantir finished 5% green today.

62419167
>>62417885
no, in 6 months

62419347
>>62417878
literal clown world valuations
if you think we're not at the tail end of the biggest financial bubble in history, prepare some rope now

62419370
so my lil soxs gonna buy me a 2nd hand bezos yacht soon? is it expensive to rename a boat? i wanna name it Tay.

62422164
>>62418985
>anyone can enter and compete in the hardware game
lol

62425452
>>62417864
Everything is slowly going to bleed and then all of a sudden, for no reason at all, Bitcoin starts pumping in october

62425502
>>62417885
>>62419167
not happening

item.title: Thinking seriously of finally capitulating and selling my 12,000 DOT at ATL of $0.80 and realizing a 100k net loss Bitco… — 21 replies — 84.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Thinking seriously of finally capitulating and selling my 12,000 DOT at ATL of $0.80 and realizing a 100k net loss Bitco…

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-06-29 06:44 UTC

OP

Thinking seriously of finally capitulating and selling my 12,000 DOT at ATL of $0.80 and realizing a 100k net loss

Bitcoin is more likely to go to 500k than dot going to $8

It's over

What do you think

Early replies (first 3)

62423104
Why on earth did you invest in that garbage in the first place?

62423107
>>62423104
My friend convinced me. Wish he was never born. Cocksucker

62423116
>>62423107
it must be traumatizing, I have seen you in like 4 different threads. And I actually do believe you, no LARP goes for this long. There will be more opportunities, maybe it's not in equities or speculative "assets" this time.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62423121
>>62423107
honestly dude this sounds insane but in the sheer absolute bumfuck rarity that DOT recovers. you're going to commit suicide if it moons. I know this sounds highly irrational but $9600 is inconsequential money unless you have debt. it sounds fucking nuts but maybe just park it safely and just genuinely forget about it.

62423122
>>62423121
>you're going to commit suicide if it moons
if you realize losses now*

this is purely psychological, it's shit financial advice and it's a gamblers fallacy but im telling you the way you're talking about this shows me if DOT moons you will not cope. It's already traumatizing missing out on the semiconductor/AI boom then having to miss the very asset you invested in recover would make ME want to blow my brains out and I have never had suicidal depression

62423140
>>62423116
>>62423121
>>62423122
Least insane DOT baggie

62423143
>>62423140
dumb misunderstanding but I get it, I am just saying 88% loss is devastating. if it was options, people just let it expire at that point

62423206
>>62423121
9600 dollar is still a huge amount to let go to zero

62423207
>>62423066
Polkadot will have a glorious comeback.

62423212
>>62423207
This confirms it's going to zero, the troll bot arrived

62423218
>>62423116
My life is ruined. Even though by the grace of God I managed to earn a good living and financially keep body and soul together, it haunts me every day. And dot is less than half of my total wreckage. We are talking 250k down the hole net loss

62423224
>>62423212
You know what's funny? I'm not even the guy who usually posts these. I just downloaded this image and started pretending to be him. I've been doing it for a few weeks now, and I've never bought a cent of DOT.

62423227
>>62423224
>shill decay to the point people are doing ironic shills
grim

62423253
>>62423227
The current 'Wendy's General' is also mine. I thought people would just make fun of it, but most took it seriously. I think that there are other ironic shills, the 'Polkadot General' wasn't mine.

62423261
>>62423212
Bear market. Remember this

62423309
>>62423261
We aren't in a bear market yet

62423332
>>62423066
Sell, treat yourself to a trip with the proceeds to unwind, and then make a fresh start with genuine assets.

62424184
>>62423066
>He didn't set a stop loss
Dumbfuck

62424714
>>62423104
Back then I recall DOT was getting a lot of support by the sorts of people that want to enslave everybody and to some cynical fucks investing in something that the people that salivate about fake crypto govie tokens that auto-track and auto-tax seemed like a safe bet.

62424732
>>62423066
Well I can tell you at very least it wont recover in the next 31 days so you can at least loss harvest for your taxes, and buy it back then. Probably at a much lower price.

Or it moons to 1k and you want to shoot me. So yea not investment advice, just saying you could if you wanted to.

62425540
>>62423066
>this is purely psychological, it's shit financial advice and it's a gamblers fallacy but im telling you the way you're talking about this shows me if DOT

Dude just DCA

Its 80 fucking cents

You can get your position cost average down 70% if you throw like 16k in it now

item.title: Nvidia will go to 0 — 20 replies — 80.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Nvidia will go to 0

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-06-29 06:38 UTC

OP

In the near future. 5 years or less. Nvidia isn't a manufacturer, and nor is it in the supply chain

It's a design company. They design chips on computers, then send the chip designs to TSMC to manufacture. Chip design was a very hard problem, which is why Nvidia needed thousands of engineers to design their chips.

The problem for Nvidia, is that AI is now beginning to design chips. The designs aren't better than Nvidia's, but it's only a matter of time. Nvidia has no moat.

Once chips are designed by AI, that takes Nvidia completely out of the loop. TSMC can just run swarms of AI in house to design chips, or other companies can design chips just as good as Nvidia with razor thin margins. Nvidia has tremendous overhead costs, they wouldn't be able to compete. I can't imagine any scenario where Nvidia continues to exist. They don't have the expertise for manufacture whih s its own equally hard problem.

This goes for AMD as well. They are a design company.

Intel. That's where you want to put your money. Intel is vertically integrated, and does their own design and manufacture. You want to put your money into the manufacturers, like TSMC, or in the physical supply chain. Avoid design and service companies like the plague.

Early replies (first 3)

62425321
>>62425311
tsmc sells shovels. they don't give a fuck about design

62425326
>>62425321
TSMC could spend peanuts on AI to pump out chip designs without Nvidia getting a cut out of sales.

62425336
>>62425326
1) this is not their business model
2) you're retarded

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62425342
>>62425311
You are running on old information. For 2-3 years now, nvidia has become a vertically integrated part of the manufacturing chain. All the big AI racks are manufactured and shipped by nvidia itself. The chips and some other parts are made elsewhere, but the final product (the NV72 systems) are all now made by nvidia.

Like many other retards, you still think of a "GPU" as a PCI card, when these days they are completely different animals:

https://www.nvidia.com/en-us/data-center/gb200-nvl72/

62425343
>>62425336
The way it works now
for every GPU that is sold
Nvidia gets a huge chunk out of what the customer paid.

If you want to be a nitpicking faggot, it doesn't have to be TSMC doing design in house. The more likely scenario is that other companies , likely small companies, move in on the design space running clusters of AI to design chips. And these companies would take razor thin margins.

I mentioned both these scenarios, but you just want to nitpick on one detail.

62425354
>>62425311
>Nvidia has tremendous overhead costs
This is also painfully retarded. Nvidia's gross margin is nearly 75%, which is insane.

62425358
>>62425342
manufacturing shelves and pipes isn't a very strong moat There's endless companies that could move in on that and take their market share.

62425363
>>62425358
They also bought Mellanox 5+ years ago, which is the world leader in the ultra high speed interconnects needed to build the huge racks. You literally do not understand the business you are "investing" in.

62425364
I knew people would misinterpret everything I said.

>>62425354
I wonder what you think would happen to Nvidia's sales if they get taken out of the loop

62425374
>>62425363
Mellanox also makes ethernet switches, and so do dozens of other companies lol

62425388
>>62425364
Taken out of the loop? Is that like "writing it off"? Do you have a definition for what "taken out of the loop" means, or is it just some vague idea in your head?

They own the chip designs, the interconnect knowledge and manufacturing, and the manufacturing of the final systems (including the huge conglomerate "superpod" systems). They take huge orders from all over the world and provide the highest perfoming tech. What incentive does any customer have for "taking them out of the loop"?

>Oh yeah guys, instead of using this company which has been giving us a nonstop flow of the highest tech available, let's dump them and start buying from random companies and putting it together ourselves. This makes complete sense.

62425416
>>62425388
It means certain companies that don't have Nvidia in the equation capture the profits that Nvidia would have taken, for themselves.

Nvidia takes a 70% margin for themselves. That can go to the manufacturers, or companies that move in with razor thin margins. Chips not made by Nvidia will cost half as much since Nvidia won't be there taking most of the margin for themselves.

62425440
>>62425416
Well I guess Ihave to bow to your brilliant thesis that the company making $89 billion a quarter with 40,000 employees is going to spontaneously fail and go to 0 when faced with....competition. No company ever survives or adjusts to competition. It's over.

62425468
>>62425440
Nvidia has multi billions in fixed operating expenses that they can't just walk out of if other companies move into the design market with minuscule operating costs.

Say 70% of the proceeds from a chip sale go to Nvidia. Nvidia can't compete against companies that are taking less than 1% out. Even if Nvidia lowers their margins to 0, they still have fixed long term expenses that they can't just get out of.

I was being a bit hyperbolic of Nvidia going to 0. They won't actually go to 0 because they have Mellanox, but they'll go to near zero lol

62425474
>>62425311
what should I short?

62425478
>>62425468
I encourage you to invest using your genius theory. Next up: Headline 1905, The Ford Motor Company is going to 0 as soon as other manufacturers start making cars cheaper!

62425491
>>62425478
that's what happened to Ford
it's exactly what happened to Ford
lol

Ford would have gone bankrupt if they weren't bailed out with billions of dollars from the US government lol

the difference between Ford and Nvidia is that Ford was deemed a critical industry. Nvidia won't be needed at all when AI an design better chips than humans.

Maye Nvidia can save itself by using the boatload of cash they have to go on buying sprees buying as many companies as they an in the chip supply chain, but as they are, with no moat, they won't make it.

62425506
>>62425491
>it's exactly what happened to Ford
You're talking about over a century later, not the next 5 years. Or are you going to start sliding your timeline?
>oh wow, a company that was on top of the world got in trouble 100 years down the road!

How about this then: "Google is going to go to zero as soon as other search engines and video streaming services start competing with them!"

62425509
>>62425478
>>62425491
And it's exactly what happened to Ford just to emphase that point again
and imagine if Ford had to compete against manufacturers that could manufacture cars for fee, and Ford still has to keep paying those union pensions
lol

that's more akin to the situation that Nvidia will be in. Ford got it easy. And Ford used to be the biggest company in the world. It's not even close to the biggest anymore.

62425522
>>62425509
>manufacture cars for fee
wtf does this mean? In what universe is nvidia suddently faced with companies that can manufacture GPUs for free, but they can't? What mechanism allows this? What other big companies are vulnerable to this free manufacturing magic? Airline manufacturers? Is Airbus going out of business because of THE POWER OF AI?

Not to mention, you listed INTEL of all fucking companies as the better bet. Even after the extreme rounds of layoffs, they still have more than 2x the number of employees nvidia has, and a revenues in the $10 billion range vs. nvidia's $89 billion. You're just a retarded bagholding shill.

item.title: how the FUCK do you get a job in this economy? — 56 replies — 61.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
how the FUCK do you get a job in this economy?

Open thread • Replies: 56 • Updated: 2026-06-29 05:53 UTC

OP

how the FUCK do you get a job in this economy?

Early replies (first 3)

62417182
know somebody

62417183
firm handshake

62417184
become a plumber

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62419800
>>62418899
I'm approaching year 4 and I've only had two dog incidents. It's always the small dogs. Big dogs may bark if their owner isn't around but odds are they are well trained and you can tell them to sit and they listen. The supervisors just tells us not to even attempt delivery if there are dogs in a yard because they don't want to do paperwork for incidents. Also mixed feelings on this, my area is converting to community mailboxes so pretty much eliminating walking. I like driving around and it's super easy to deliver to one spot but I'll have to start doing more cardiovascular exercise outside of work.

62419806
>>62417164
Put nigger on your CV. Under experiences write police brutality. The femoids in HR will pay you to go work there.

62420387
>>62418796
I'm a train driver and feel like you guys work harder. Would love to be a postman on a postie bike if trains get automated here.

62420535
>>62419102
>It's likely all online job listings are fake
At least 90% are and the few remaining get hundreds of applicants so you're guaranteed to be competing with PHDs for 18 bucks an hour work.

62420580
>>62417275
Dont want to discourage anyone, maybe its just me.
i have 10 years in aerospace eng., took me 16 months to find a job. Have sent thousands of applications, hundreds of linkedin dms. Even some cold calls (actually cold calling worked best). Granted the new job is FAANG but requires relocation.

62420599
>>62417164
Be brown and pay an Indian college for a fake degree. You might be able to get a job at a Walmart then.

62420653
>>62418900
i think a big part of the sub culture of 4chan is introverts. you have to talk to people, like in real life. getting a mcjob trade job is a lot easier these days, getting a decent tech job not so much.

otherwise fake being an incompetent LGBTWFTBBQ, big companies love that shit

62420692
This is the new normal, I fear.

62420767
>>62417182
Truke

62420776
>>62417275
>I’m honestly confused how you guys have so much trouble.
14 years gap in my resume, it's the kind of gap you can't come back from

62421717
>>62417164
You don't. You just stay in the basement till your parents lose it all then move to skidrow.

62421753
>>62417164
backstory behind OPs pic?

62421810
>>62421753
imagine working and a nigger messes with you

62421837
>>62420387
Here in Western Canada, we still have trapped time in our contract. I can either get my 8 hour day done in 3 hours and leave or do overtime without actually physically working more than 8 hours.

62421957
>>62421753
When wondering about the backstory behind a video, always ask
>who was recording?
Note especially the perfectly placed camera at the optimal distance to capture the event at just the right time.

62422003
>>62421957
yes that's what happens when nogs start recording you to try and get you fired so they can get a payday

62422125
>>62417164
just learn to code bro :)

62422331
>>62419033
Skilled trades have at least another 20 years. Electrician/Plumber robots would be far too expensive to mass produce /maintain. Robots would get wrecked badly on a construction site. Cheaper to pay some poor stiff decent money to complete this type of work.

62425015
>>62417275
>I’m an accountant
What certs did you get, if any?

62425187
>>62418900
you're hired
get to licking

62425218
It is brutal out there. People who once called each other friends are turning on one another over a few dollars. Loans go unpaid, promises are broken, and trust is treated like a luxury few can afford. When survival becomes the priority, integrity is often the first casualty. Desperation can make people justify actions they would have condemned in better times, convincing themselves that looking after their own interests is all that matters.
This doesn't mean everyone has lost their moral compass, but it does mean hardship reveals character. Some people become more generous under pressure; others become more self-serving. The painful reality is that financial stress exposes the strength—or weakness—of relationships. A friend who disappears when it's time to repay a debt or honor their word wasn't tested by prosperity, but by adversity.

In times like these, blind trust becomes expensive. Kindness is still worth practicing, but it should be paired with discernment. Help where you can, lend only what you can afford to lose, and judge people by their actions rather than their promises. The world may feel harsher than before, but protecting your integrity while setting healthy boundaries is one of the few things entirely within your control.

62425245
>>62417247
be indian / shit your pants

62425246
In today's world, the landscape feels ruthlessly cutthroat, where trust is scarce and survival instincts dominate. Friendships are fractured by greed, with friends betraying friends over petty gains, each driven by a relentless desire to secure a penny at the expense of loyalty. The pressure pushes individuals into a full-fledged survival mode, where debts are ignored and promises broken, revealing a society driven by self-interest rather than integrity. Siblings, once bound by blood, now turn against each other, succumbing to irrational impulses and greed, resulting in familial culls that devastate bonds and shatter trust. The marketplace is flooded with counterfeit goods—fake food, fake meat, counterfeit watches, and deceptive websites—all designed to deceive and exploit. Markets are manipulated through fake floods, artificially inflating prices and creating chaos, while consumers are misled into buying fake products that threaten health and trust. The proliferation of fake jobs and deceptive online platforms further muddies the waters, making it difficult for genuine opportunities and authentic connections to thrive. This deluge of fakery reflects a society that has lost its moral compass, where authenticity is sacrificed for profit, and survival hinges on deception. It’s a brutal, chaotic environment where integrity is a rare commodity, and every deal, friendship, or transaction could be a facade hiding ulterior motives. In this climate, trust is a luxury, and the real winners are those who master the art of deception and adaptation, leaving behind a world increasingly devoid of genuine human connection and honesty.

62425249
Go into mamual labor or wash dishes.
>NOOOOOOOOOOO
Then stop bitching about no one hiring retard.

62425252
nepotism

you want to know how people get rich? they were born rich

62425270
>>62417164
i once heard a fat woman say "i got my cousin a job at walmart but she be stealin'." so i guess you need to have a cousin that works at walmart.

62425309
>>62417164
don't be a retard pothead

62425320
>White men are being removed from the job market because they have zero skills besides being a blue light vampire in a cubicle.

The absolute state of white men

62425330
>>62425249
i got fired from dishwashing it isnt easy interacting with normies

62425382
>>62425320
> the real winners are those who master the art of deception and adaptation
Jew always win

OP

>now we know the price floor is $150
now is the time to buy SPCX

62408493
>>62408480

62408507
>>62408493
No cope saar only gains to be made! Please buy more stocks from this very profitable company! Time is limited for maximum gains must hurry before money train takes off!

62408519
>>62408480
>>only down 4% from its ipo,
kek about to 50% in 6 months

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62414121
>>62408480
with only 5% of the float available or w/e. holy shit yall are fucked when it opens up

62414137
>>62408480
Melon got re-bogged and fattened which he only does when things are going badly for him
2x short because of this

62414157
>>62408480
>We privatized NASA and now we want you to pay us to see Hollywood for scientists productions
Yeah, nah. I saw Kubrick admit to faking the moon landing.

62414178
>>62408480
There's genuinely not enough data to make a principled model of it. However the only thing not hypey about SpaceX is StarLink, which is a genuine business. Merging xAI into SpaceX however just tied a 1 ton ball and chain around it, because it's an insatiable blackhole of capex with no clear upside.

62415681
>>62414137
he was spending too much time being a master of POE2 instead of going to the gym. he'll get back on the horse.

62415707
This thread didn't age well.

62415715
>>62415707
$150 is still the floor. it'll be $300 by hanukkah

62415763
>>62411812
Holy baggie, this garbage isn't worth a fraction of what it is, why would your delusional number be even close to the floor

62416005
>>62408480
90x earnings is INSANE. Investing in this is a sure marker you are retarded and willful exit liquidity. Equivalent to buying Worldcomm with the exception that Space X probably has a longterm future, but is at absolute bubble top with an ABSURD valuation and no course to revenues in the near future, kept aloft by virtue of its multiple rules changed inclusion in indices receiving passive flows and retail hype that has come and gone.

62416247
>>62416005
>>62415763
it's already up for the day!

62416343
>>62416005
>Investing in this is a sure marker you are retarded and willful exit liquidity

while true, never underestimate the retardation of the retail masses

62416471
>>62408480
Keep buy Bagholders, keep buying!

62416737
>>62408480
I haven't seen a single bull "case" that has used numbers for buying Spcx. Meanwhile all Bears have to do is look at that those Quarterly and Y/Y financials directly below OP's image they were conveniently edited out.

62416873
>>62415707
Yep people of /biz/ seem to be unable to value this asset correctly. The SpaceMarines are back!!

62417077
doubters in shambles

62417086
AI FUCKING DATA CENTERS IN SPACE!

62417121
>>62408480
IT JUST KEEPS TUMBLIMG DOWN TUMBLIMG DOWN
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oIscL-Bjsq4

BAGGIES ARE YOU OK
BAGGIES ARE YOU OK
ARE YOU OK BAGGIES


Did you guys manage to sell in thegreen be honest?

62417135
>>62408480
ITS OVER

62417520
>>62417077
Yep, truly in shambles. Another down day.

Even if SpaceX is an amazing compnay, entry point matters. SpaceX market cap at $1 trillion is when it starts to get somewhat attractive. Just be patient young Padawon.

62418371
>>62408480
>only down 4% from its ipo, which is very reasonable
okay so you expected 30% by now... or what..... what is this point? this isnt even cope it's just weird

62419735
>>62408480
The price floor was always $150.
If it's below that at (can't remember date) they can't sell more stock.
So it's got to be $150 by that date. Someone can find it. Don't care.

62419742
isn't most of retail locked in until precisely the point where early investors are legally allowed to sell and cash in at any price? oh yes they are.

62419746
>>62414178
the listing has a TAM of equivalent of the total value of the us economy, or the the money spent on food by the planet, every year, year on year, to pay for its ai services. are elons data centres worth more than the entire us economy, or the entire global food chain supply, now or in the future?

62419817
>>62419746
Qualifier.
Do I live in a machine in the future?

62419957
OP bought the top and is now panicking to sell the bottom

62420705
>>62419957
This is the vibe I'm getting. I really hope you guys didn't see the most obvious pump and dump scheme in history and still fall for it like an indian who can see the train coming yet still gets hit by it.

62421234
Imagine when starship fails, imagine when you can dump without IPO penalty.

62421347
>>62419957
not even remotely close to the bottom

62422910
>>62417520
More like $200bn MC,

62425386
>>62408480

item.title: hey linkies. did you miss me. just got back from another long luxurious vacation paid for by my NON-chainlink crypto gai… — 11 replies — 44.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
hey linkies. did you miss me. just got back from another long luxurious vacation paid for by my NON-chainlink crypto gai…

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-06-29 06:36 UTC

OP

hey linkies. did you miss me. just got back from another long luxurious vacation paid for by my NON-chainlink crypto gains. and what a delicious treat to see when i returned: link struggling to stay above $7 and fat serg canceling the build program. LMAO. you stinkoids just can't catch a break huh. honestly how do ya'll have any hope left at this point. what is going to cause link to break $10, let alone $50? kekkerinos.

Early replies (first 3)

62424357
>>62424306
We've missed you. :(

62424375
After a big pump and dump, assets seldom pump again. It happened once with BTC but it's rare. Link is unlikely to see serious gains again.

62424382
>>62424375
The big banks will pump our bags once they start using LINK in earnest by 2030 to run the entire financial infrastructure.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62424407
>>62424306
Laugh all you want, but the linkies and their friends will be here any minute.

62424429
>>62424407

62424443

62425371

62425397
>>62424306

>>62424375
>>62424382
>>62424407

Fellow link holders we should just ignore this guy.

He is spreading fud about our investment and he is wrong

Can't the mods just ban him?

I mean I know this is 4chan and all and we like to joke and be mean to one another but as a multiple year long chainlink holder I have my limits

Anyway i'm back to my job cleaning toilets now

62425406
>>62424382
i find great satisfction that there are people like you who never took any profit at all...

i get great pleasure knowing it just gets worse and worse for you as time goes on,


i get great pleasure knowing your lasts breaths will be that you sadly had to pass away before link pumped

62423149

62423165
>>62423149
I did. This thread is an ad. I paid in sweat & time. Eternal currencies.


Now help me, and yourself, make money (stack XMR) or fuck off

62423660

pool's closed
now what?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62423761
>>62423660

62423780
>>62423761
I don't know how
they are using AI to monitor everyone's transactions to make sure we aren't buying illicit things
>I'm just but humble bottom in this sexual encounter

62424046

62424688

Just don't be a cuck

62425467

item.title: Have you made peace with BTC going to zero? — 12 replies — 10.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Have you made peace with BTC going to zero?

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-06-29 05:42 UTC

OP

Have you made peace with BTC going to zero? The reasons are simple: BTC has no cash flows, no redemption value, no sovereign backing, no productive asset underneath it; its price depends on belief in future buyers; the digital-gold narrative could fail; governments can strangle exchanges, ETFs, banks, custodians, miners, and payment rails; institutional adoption can become institutional dumping; miners may become underpaid as halvings reduce rewards; falling hashpower can make attacks cheaper; a 51% attack or repeated reorgs could destroy settlement confidence; mining can become politically toxic because of energy use; mining can concentrate in vulnerable jurisdictions or pools; quantum computing could threaten exposed wallets; the quantum fix could require an ugly fork or coin freeze; Satoshi-era coins could move or be compromised; a catastrophic protocol bug could break trust; governance paralysis could prevent necessary upgrades; fork wars could fracture the Schelling point of “the real Bitcoin”; chain surveillance and blacklisting could ruin fungibility; lack of privacy could push serious users elsewhere; .stablecoins can eat the payments use case;

Early replies (first 3)

62424965
gold, equities, tokenized bonds, or newer crypto assets can eat the store-of-value use case; scams, ransomware, and illicit-use narratives can make BTC politically radioactive; major custodians, exchanges, or ETFs can fail; self-custody can remain too hard for normal people; lost coins can make liquidity brittle; fees can become either too high for users or too low for miners; Lightning and other scaling layers can fail to matter; developers, ASIC makers, pools, custodians, and exchanges can become legal chokepoints; the culture can age out; the 21-million-supply meme can lose its magic; hostile macro conditions can make non-yielding BTC unattractive; leverage can unwind violently; liquidity can vanish; and once enough people stop believing that everyone else will keep believing, the whole monetary coordination game can collapse into novelty-token value.

62425009
>>62424963
9 months from ATH and you're posting this so you can buy lower? it's still got years to bleed, i wouldn't buy now op, you know it's on a 4 year cycle right?

62425016
>>62424963
We are above 60k bofaggot.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62425021
genuinely don't care about fud I just buy some every month until they stop hyperinflating (they won't

62425050
>>62425021
AI + robotics will create deflation. But good luck with your digital nothingness.

62425054
>>62425016
Thanks for the entry liquidity ʕ•ᴥ•ʔ

62425059
>>62425050
Deflation? Hearty kek

62425072
>>62425054
ʕ•ᴥ•ʔʕ•ᴥ•ʔʕ•ᴥ•ʔʕ•ᴥ•ʔʕ•ᴥ•ʔ
fukken saved

62425073
>>62425054
lmao you're seriously shorting the bottom?

62425088
I think it won’t go to zero but it could fall very far. I think there are still a few darknet markets that will let you use it instead of monero, so I’m sure there will still be some value linked to it.

62425089
>>62425072
God I wanna fuck this bobina so bad. I love her chubbiness

62425345
>>62424963
I want to buy lower too, this guy is right!

item.title: VOO and Chill — 144 replies — 29.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
VOO and Chill

Open thread • Replies: 144 • Updated: 2026-06-29 01:56 UTC

OP

The more I look into investing and compare the historical returns of certain ETFs / individual stocks it seems like the whole philosophy of “VOO and Chill” is equal parts really smart and retarded
It’s like a no brainer for money but even now as long as the market is in a growth period shouldn’t you at least do QQQ?
It seems so simple that’d you miss a lot of upside but the returns are honestly not bad and extremely consistent
How accurate is this assessment? It’s both so simple and guaranteed that everyone should it but also too safe so you end up missing out on more upside
I’m talking 5-20 years investing here not trading

Early replies (first 3)

62400515
Boomer funds are gay and lame

62400554
>>62400515
What qualifies as one
Why
What crypto do you want to shill

62400560
boglehead method is the tried and tested way, buy the market with an index fund that has a low fee, invest monthly, do it consistently for decades and you WILL be rich
but biz/anons don't care about being rich when they're older, they want to be rich NOW
greed is a deadly sin

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62421799
>"just VOO and chill bro"
>gets caught in a 5+ yr -60% drawdown event (2008) with no hedges to cover his balls
boyhood is regurgitating the VOO mantra
adulthood is meticulously engineering your poorfolio to get within 80% of index performance while cutting the drawdown periods by half

62421813
>>62400493
Or I can throw a good amount into Uranium miner indexes and chill to retire in half the time

62422318
>>62419913
>I even did completely mong shit like having a python script take a screenshot of every known stock loaded into eval sites like GuruFocus etc and then simply counting the green pixel ratio.
kek

62422806
>>62422766
i use a modified barbell strategy that i call "dick and balls" method.

>40% high risk high return ETFs
this is your right nut and drives your explosive folio growth. you want trending sector or thematic ETFs here.

>40% value ETFs with low low beta
this is your left nut and provides slow, steady growth even during volatile conditions. stuff like SCHD would fall in here.

>20% ultrasafe assets like bonds and treasurys
this is your shaft, without which your balls have no raison d'etre. this will grow steadily as you grow older and provide the foundation upon which your entire portfolio stands erect.

when you're young, you take ballsy risks, hence the 40-40 balls.
as you get older, you seek wisdom and steady upwards thrust, hence your 20% shaft grows year by year.

use a portfolio backtester/optimizer to determine the exact composition of each component. you can get FAR less max drawdown than plain S&P while retaining most of returns. the right nut can be configured to catch seasonal upsides, i.e. whatever's hottest at the moment.

62423809
Using a reversion to the mean strategy on indexes, I managed to get a 17% CAGR over 54 years only trading indexes/ETFs (where data permits) on backtesting, significantly beating each individual index . More enquiry is warranted but I think "VOO and chill" can be improved upon and optimized.

>>62423719

62423875
>>62400636
It actually is something tho. $150 a month becomes 170k at 7% real returns for 30 years. This gives you an extra $570/mo in retirement.

Is that a LOT? Fuck no. But we’re talking poorfag standards where social security is the only thing you have. An extra 500-600 a month is a significant increase over base SS for a poorfag especially if you move to an ultra low cost of living area.
